concepts.title: The theme of the novel
concepts.description: The novel 'Indigo' by Thomas Thompson revolves around the theme of the effects of industrialization on the rural community and the consequences of commercialization of crops, specifically indigo, which leads to the displacement of the traditional farmers and artisans.

concepts.title: The role of the narrator
concepts.description: The narrator of the novel acts as the spokesperson for the people of the rural community, highlighting their struggles and plight, making the readers empathize with their situation.

concepts.title: The significance of the title 'Indigo'
concepts.description: The title 'Indigo' symbolizes the crop that is central to the novel, representing the struggle of the rural community against the commercialization of their land and the loss of their traditional way of life.

concepts.title: Identifying the industrialist and the farmer
concepts.description: To differentiate between the industrialist and the farmer, remember that the industrialist is the one who prioritizes profits and commercialization, while the farmer is the one who emphasizes the traditional and sustainable methods of farming.

concepts.title: Confusing the narrator's tone
concepts.description: Students often confuse the narrator's tone, mistaking it for a neutral or objective tone. However, the narrator's tone is actually sympathetic and biased towards the rural community, making the readers aware of the injustices they face.

concepts.title: The consequences of industrialization
concepts.description: The novel highlights the consequences of industrialization, including the displacement of traditional farmers, the destruction of the rural community's way of life, and the loss of their cultural heritage.

concepts.title: The author's purpose
concepts.description: The author, Thomas Thompson, aims to expose the negative consequences of industrialization and commercialization, highlighting the struggle of the rural community and emphasizing the importance of preserving traditional methods of farming and the cultural heritage of the community.

concepts.title: The character of the narrator
concepts.description: The narrator is a representative of the rural community, who shares their experiences, struggles, and emotions, making the readers aware of the injustices they face and the consequences of industrialization.
